title: .gitignore
content:
### What is .gitignore?

A `.gitignore` file tells Git which files or directories to ignore — they won't be tracked or shown as untracked.

### Basic Patterns

```gitignore
# Comments start with #

node_modules/          # Ignore entire directory
*.log                  # Ignore all .log files
build/                 # Ignore build output
.env                   # Ignore a specific file
.DS_Store              # macOS metadata

# Negation — don't ignore even if pattern matches
!important.log

# Ignore files in any directory
dist/

# Ignore only at root
/config.json
```

### Global .gitignore

Create a global ignore file for your machine (applies to all repos):

```bash
git config --global core.excludesFile ~/.gitignore_global
```

Common global ignores: `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`, editor swap files.

### Ignoring Already Tracked Files

If a file is already tracked, `.gitignore` won't affect it. Stop tracking it:

```bash
git rm --cached file.txt           # Remove from tracking but keep on disk
git rm --cached -r build/          # Remove directory from tracking
```

Then add it to `.gitignore`.

### .gitignore Templates

GitHub provides templates for various languages and frameworks:
- https://github.com/github/gitignore

```bash
# Download a template
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/gitignore/main/Node.gitignore
```
codeExample:
# .gitignore
node_modules/
dist/
.env
*.log
.DS_Store

# Stop tracking an already tracked file
git rm --cached .env
echo ".env" >> .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git commit -m "Stop tracking .env"

quiz.question: What does git rm --cached do?
options: Removes file from tracking without deleting it

quiz.question: How do you set a global .gitignore?
